title: REIT Fantasy Football: 11 Companies For Your Starting Lineup
text: REIT valuations are down and yields are up, making them attractive to income investors again. The article suggests a diversified REIT portfolio for beginners, with one REIT from each sector, to form a REIT fantasy football team. Different types of REITs serve different roles, including stable income, growth potential, and niche opportunities.

title: Lessons Learned: Adversity Is Bitter But Its Uses May Be Sweet
text: I reflect on my past experiences as a real estate developer and the financial collapse I faced. Investing in quality properties that provide long-term value is of utmost importance. Emphasis must be placed on the significance of quality balance sheets and management teams in successful investing.

title: Here's How Simon Property Group Can Afford Its 6.6% Dividend Yield
text: Simon is benefiting from a robust labor market and strong consumer spending. The dividend is well covered.

title: Simon Property Group: The Comeback Dividend
text: The Simon Property Group, Inc. dividend is still recovering from the 2020 cut. But the dividend is not yet back to 2019 levels. Simon Property Group has a high debt rating and its diversification and superior locations have helped it bounce back better than competitors. The dividend yield is high and the payout ratio is historically low. This is a great bargain combination for investors. Dividend increases are expected to continue.

title: 2 High-Dividend Retail Stocks I Never Plan to Sell
text: Don't make the mistake of thinking retail is dead.

title: Simon Property Group Continues To Have Strong Opportunity
text: Simon Property Group, Inc. is one of the largest REITs in the U.S., with a market capitalization of nearly $40 billion. The company has generated strong financial results, with revenue of $1.37 billion in the most recent quarter and net income of $560 million. Simon Property Group's portfolio includes premium properties that have performed well despite the downturn in malls, and the company continues to invest in its business for future growth.

title: 5 REITs That Will Likely Hike Their Dividends
text: REITs are historically cheap due to high interest rates. But the high inflation is leading to rapid rent growth. Here are 5 undervalued REITs that are likely to hike their dividend.

title: Despite Commercial Real Estate Worries, Simon Property's Business Remains Strong
text: The economy remains strong overall, which supports Simon Property Group. Earnings missed Wall Street estimates, but that just made the valuation more attractive.

title: 3 REIT Stocks For Your August 2023 Watchlist
text: 3 REIT stocks to watch this month.

title: The Falls Debuts Miami's First-Of-Its-Kind, 120,000-Square-Foot Life Time Athletic Country Club on August 4
text: Simon's premier shopping center now home to the ultimate in health and wellness with outdoor beach club, salt water pools, indoor pickleball and fitness spaces, kids academy, cafe, spa and more MIAMI , Aug. 4, 2023 /PRNewswire/ -- In a first for Miami's shopping center scene, Life Time (NYSE: LTH), the nation's premier healthy lifestyle brand, today debuted its one-of-a-kind, athletic country club experience at The Falls, a Simon property. The opening marks the Company's second location in greater Miami and fourth in South Florida as it continues its nationwide expansion.

title: Simon Property (SPG) Lags Q2 FFO, Ups Dividend & '23 Outlook
text: While Simon Property's (SPG) Q2 results reflect better-than-anticipated revenues, higher operating expenses and interest expenses act as dampeners.

title: Simon Property Group: The Yield Will Get Fatter
text: Simon Property Group just hiked its quarterly dividend by 2.7% for an annualized forward dividend yield of 6.1%. The REIT is set to cover its annualized dividend by 157% with fiscal 2023 funds from operations. A fortress balance sheet that held cash and equivalents of $1.29 billion as of the end of its fiscal 2023 second quarter is set to support further dividend hikes.

title: Simon Property (SPG) Q2 Earnings: Taking a Look at Key Metrics Versus Estimates
text: While the top- and bottom-line numbers for Simon Property (SPG) give a sense of how the business performed in the quarter ended June 2023, it could be worth looking at how some of its key metrics compare to Wall Street estimates and year-ago values.

title: Simon Property (SPG) Q2 FFO Miss Estimates
text: Simon Property (SPG) came out with quarterly funds from operations (FFO) of $2.88 per share, missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $2.91 per share. This compares to FFO of $2.96 per share a year ago.

title: Sell Alert: 3 Real Estate Stocks to Dump ASAP
text: Real estate stocks are forming the perfect storm. First, the pandemic greatly reduced society's desire to use various kinds of commercial real estate.

title: One REIT To Sell And One REIT To Buy
text: Most REITs are today priced at historically low valuations. But that does not mean that all REITs are worth buying. I highlight one REIT that I would sell and a superior alternative.
